source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Unintentional discharge of synthetic oil-based mud into the Gulf of Mexico
actions_taken: Shut down the #1 mud mixing pump
Strapped the #11 and #13 mud pits to determine discharge volume
Revised and implemented Job Safety Analysis (JSA) with Fluid Transfer Pre-Checklist
Conducted documented discussion with on and off tour Derrickhands
event.summary: On February 27, 2021, approximately 13 barrels of synthetic oil-based mud (SOBM) containing 6.24 barrels of synthetic oil were inadvertently discharged into the Gulf of Mexico during a wash water overboard transfer operation on the Diamond Ocean Black Hornet drilling rig. The discharge resulted from improper valve alignment at the mud mix manifold combined with inadequate communication during a tour change. The #1 mud mixing pump was left running while circulating mud in the #11 and #13 active mud pits, but this critical information was not properly communicated to the incoming crew. When the overboard discharge valve was opened, mud was discharged instead of wash water. The incident was detected when the Driller noticed volume loss in the active mud pits and immediately notified the Derrickhands, who shut down the pump and closed the valve.
recommendations: Implement Fluid Transfer Pre-Checklist to ensure both mud mixing pumps are switched off prior to valve alignment
Improve handover documentation and communication procedures during tour changes
Ensure proper line-up checks by walking the line to confirm appropriate valves are open and others are closed prior to opening the overboard discharge valve

hazards.name: Pressurized mud system with active circulation pump
Synthetic oil-based mud (SOBM) in containment system

threats.name: Improper valve alignment at mud mix manifold
Mud mixing pump left running during tour change
Inadequate communication of equipment status during shift handover

consequences.name: Discharge of synthetic oil-based mud into Gulf of Mexico
Environmental pollution from base oil release

controls.name: Transfer Permitted Fluid Overboard Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to prevent improper valve alignment
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural safeguard for fluid transfer operations

supporting_text: The Diamond Offshore Transfer Permitted Fluid Overboard procedure states to double check the line up by walking the line to confirm appropriate valves are open and others are closed prior to opening the overboard discharge valve.
The crew 1 Derrickhands failed to properly check the mud system and valve alignments prior to opening the overboard discharge valve.

controls.name: Handover logs and verbal notifications during tour change
controls.barrier_role: Communication control to ensure equipment status awareness
controls.lod_basis: Primary communication safeguard during shift transitions

supporting_text: The mixing pump activation was not listed in the daily handover logs given to either of the crew 1 Derrickhands coming on tour.

During the tour change meeting, one of the crew 1 Derrickhands one was informed that the #1 mud mixing pump was still circulating the #11 and #13 mud pits, and the other was informed that the mud weight was increased but not specifically told that the #1 mud mixing pump was still running.
Poor communication during tour change also contributed to this discharge.

controls.name: Mud volume monitoring and Driller observation
controls.barrier_role: Detection and response control to identify discharge
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection barrier for identifying abnormal mud losses

supporting_text: The Driller instantly noticed mud volume losses in the #11 and #13 mud pits after they opened the overboard discharge valve and he notified the Derrickhands.
They referenced the mud monitoring screen and shut down the #1 mud mixing pump and closed the discharge valve, but not before discharging 13 barrels of SOBM into the Gulf of Mexico.

controls.name: Static sheen test prior to discharge
controls.barrier_role: Quality assurance control for wash water discharge
controls.lod_basis: Verification control for permitted discharge fluid

supporting_text: The Mud Engineer performed a static sheen test as required prior to discharging the wash water in the #17 mud pit with no abnormalities noted.

controls.name: Fluid Transfer Pre-Checklist (implemented post-incident)
controls.barrier_role: Enhanced procedural control to verify pump status before valve alignment
controls.lod_basis: Preventive measure to ensure mud mixing pumps are off before operations

supporting_text: Diamond Offshore has revised and implemented their Job Safety Analysis (JSA) and the Site-Specific Plan (SSP), adding a Fluid Transfer Pre-Checklist that includes a step to ensure both mud mixing pumps are switched off prior to the valve alignment.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
